Method–provenance summary
Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions (f⁺ electrophilic, f⁻ nucleophilic, f⁰ dual) and identification of the most reactive sites in aniline.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier “standard”); exact four-center integrals (density fitting not employed).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied aniline.xyz (14 atoms, path “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz”).  No further optimization; single-point calculations performed on this fixed geometry (DFT//input-xyz protocol).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Electronic state: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (parent).  N–1 cation: charge +1, multiplicity 2; N+1 anion: charge –1, multiplicity 2.
Software: PySCF 22.1 via chemkit “fukui-reactivity” skill (engine subcommand fukui).
SCF convergence: all three states converged; integrity.status = "ok", trustworthy = true.
